text: Deep learning has gone through a series of revolutions and now is the time to harvest its fruits on graphs. Graphs are a class of non-Euclidean data that encodes highly complex relationships between different objects and are therefore ubiquitous in today’s machine learning applications. This post aims at giving a detailed view of the way we can transform a number of important use cases such as text, entities, and relationships into graphs and then leverage Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to predict things. It focuses on the principles of GNNs by looking into the details of the message-passing algorithms used in them and takes a closer look at famous GNNs such as Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs), GraphSAGE and Graph Attention Networks (GATs). It also reviews the state of the art of GNNs research along with popular datasets, libraries and implementation across multiple fields of computer science.
explanation: The passage presents a coherent, high-level overview with generic phrasing and without distinctive personal details or unusual specificity. This style is common in AI-generated text, though it could also be human-written; there is no strong signature of either.
